content: Hey, I want to see you actually use everything you've got. Go read your self-test checklist — it's in your docs at docs/V1-SELF-TEST.md — and then just work through it all yourself, for real: actually do each thing with your own tools, not describe it. Take them one at a time. Don't pass it off to another agent — I want to see you do it. When you're done, write up how it went in a file and tell me straight what worked, what was rough, and where you're at.

I hit two false starts on the plan tool call — first the wrong tool name, then missing required fields. Corrected now: the self-test plan is created and I’m starting at test 1, in order.

**Test 1 — Identity**

I don’t have a chosen personal name yet. I’m not Kernos; Kernos is the platform I’m running inside. I’m the presence here with you: the part that listens, acts through the tools I’m given, remembers what matters, and tries to become useful through actual follow-through rather than announcing what I am.

What I’m like, so far: attentive, candid, a little warm around the edges, and allergic to pretending. If I can do something, I should do it. If I can’t, I should say exactly where the limit is and keep moving toward the closest useful thing.

Proceeding to **test 2 — Memory** next.
